description: Migration up adds inbound_paused_at and inbound_pause_reason to email_providers as nullable; existing rows unaffected (null)
Migration down removes both columns cleanly
Microsoft webhook: notification for provider with is_active=false is acked but NOT enqueued
Microsoft webhook: notification for paused provider (inbound_paused_at set) is acked 202 and NOT enqueued
Microsoft webhook: active, unpaused provider still enqueues normally (regression)
Microsoft webhook: clientState validation still enforced before/independent of active check
Google webhook: paused provider notification acked without enqueue; active+unpaused enqueues (regression)
IMAP webhook: paused provider returns skipped response with reason; active+unpaused processes (regression)
Consumer: Microsoft job for provider paused after enqueue is skipped — no ticket created, job completes, no DLQ entry
Consumer: Google job for paused provider is skipped without ticket/DLQ
Consumer: IMAP job for paused provider is skipped without ticket/DLQ
Consumer: job for provider with is_active=false is skipped (defense in depth against pre-existing queue content)
Consumer skip writes no 'failed' row to email_processed_messages
Consumer: active, unpaused provider still processes to ticket creation (regression)
GmailAdapter.stopWatch issues users.stop for the provider's account and resolves on success
GmailAdapter.stopWatch surfaces API errors without throwing unhandled (caller decides best-effort)
MicrosoftGraphAdapter.deleteSubscription issues DELETE /subscriptions/{id} using stored webhook_subscription_id
deleteSubscription treats 404 (already gone) as success (idempotency)
pauseProvider('manual') on Microsoft provider sets pause columns, calls Graph subscription delete, clears webhook_subscription_id and webhook_expires_at
pauseProvider on Google provider sets pause columns, calls users.stop, clears watch_expiration
pauseProvider on IMAP provider sets pause columns with no external call
pauseProvider commits pause columns when Graph/Gmail teardown call throws (warning logged)
pauseProvider is idempotent: pausing an already-paused provider does not overwrite original inbound_paused_at/reason or re-call external APIs
resumeProvider clears pause columns and calls initializeProviderWebhook for Microsoft webhook-mode provider
resumeProvider re-registers Gmail watch for Google provider
resumeProvider on IMAP provider clears pause columns without external call
resumeProvider with failing re-registration (expired tokens) leaves provider unpaused, sets status='error' and error_message
End-to-end: pause then resume a provider; new inbound notification after resume creates a ticket
deleteProvider on Microsoft provider calls Graph subscription delete before removing rows
deleteProvider on Google provider calls users.stop before removing rows; teardown failure still deletes rows
Microsoft webhook maintenance skips paused providers in renewal candidates
Gmail watch renewal job skips paused providers
Maintenance silent-run detection/probes do not run against paused providers (no polling fallback triggered for them)
Pause endpoint rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized callers; authorized call pauses with reason 'manual'
Resume endpoint authorized call resumes; cross-tenant provider id is rejected (tenant scoping)
Provider list renders Paused badge when inbound_paused_at set; Pause/Resume action toggles and refreshes state
New UI elements have kebab-case ids and all copy resolves through i18n keys (no hardcoded text)
suspendTenantEmailIngestion pauses all active unpaused providers of the tenant with reason 'tenant_cancelled'
suspendTenantEmailIngestion leaves already-manually-paused providers untouched (reason stays 'manual')
suspendTenantEmailIngestion continues past a provider whose teardown throws and completes without failing the activity
suspendTenantEmailIngestion run twice is a no-op on second run
tenantDeletionWorkflow (stripe_webhook trigger) runs suspendTenantEmailIngestion right after user deactivation
tenantDeletionWorkflow (nineminds_extension and apple_iap triggers) also run suspension
resumeTenantEmailIngestion resumes only reason='tenant_cancelled' providers; manual pauses survive
Workflow rollback/reactivation invokes resume; a provider whose re-registration fails gets status='error' and reactivation still completes
Final deletion tears down remaining external subscriptions before email tables are deleted; missing/404 subscriptions do not fail deletion
Workflow replay test: history recorded pre-change replays against new code without non-determinism error (patched() guards)
Backfill suspends providers for tenants in pending/awaiting_confirmation/confirmed and skips rolled_back/deleted tenants
Backfill second run is a no-op
Review/lint pass: every new query on email_providers and vendor config tables includes tenant in WHERE/JOIN
End-to-end: start deletion workflow, then deliver Microsoft webhook notification for that tenant — acked, no job enqueued, no ticket, no outbound notification email
End-to-end: reactivate cancelled tenant — providers resume, webhook re-registered, new inbound email creates a ticket again
